title: Hero — the home-page cover (offer + capacity + clock-aware greeting)
problem: The Hero is the home page's magazine cover — the visitor's first paragraph of contact with the practice. It carries the offer headline, a live capacity readout (1 active + 1 in triage = open or paused), a HeroShippedProject card linking to the latest showcased work, and a clock-aware greeting (late-night gets a dry 'encore là?' because most honest traffic happens after 22h). Capacity is fetched from /api/capacity at request time — visitor sees the truth, not a marketing claim.
analysis: Renders DICT[lang].hero copy. Two live data sources fetched on mount: getCapacityLive (returns the current 1+1 cap state for the badge) and listPublicProjects (top 1 for HeroShippedProject). Clock-aware greeting via pickSalut(lang) — reads visitor's local clock and picks the right greeting (morning / day / evening / late-night). Late-night gets the dry Québécois 'encore là?' which is the anti-AI-tells anchor — no AI would write that line. Feature colour is undefined (Hero crosses every feature).

decisions.decision: Capacity is fetched live, not a static claim
decisions.rationale: Saying 'I take 1 active build at a time' is a structural claim — it must be testable. Showing the LIVE state ('currently building X for Y, triage queue is open / closed') turns it from claim into observation. Capacity cap race fix (AUDIT P1.7) makes this trustworthy.
decisions.recommendation: Don't switch to static copy. The live readout IS the trust statement.

decisions.rationale: Default greeting + late-night variant. Late-night ('encore là?') is the Québécois register tell — no chatbot would write that. Marc's family-first practice has a late-night honest-traffic pattern (visitors browsing after kid-bedtime); the greeting acknowledges it.
decisions.recommendation: Don't translate 'encore là?' to anything generic. The literal Québécois is the load-bearing word.

successCriteria:
- Renders offer headline + capacity badge + HeroShippedProject + greeting
- Capacity readout reflects live D1 state
- Greeting varies with visitor's local clock
- Late-night greeting in Québécois register
- Bilingual at full parity
- Two live fetches on mount, no waterfall
implementation: src/components/Hero.tsx — useEffect fetches getCapacityLive + listPublicProjects in parallel. pickSalut(lang) returns the greeting string based on new Date().getHours(). useLangSwitch hook handles the FR/EN toggle in-page.
userGuide: Edit headline copy via DICT.fr.hero / DICT.en.hero. To adjust greeting time windows: pickSalut in the file. To change the capacity badge wording: same file. The HeroShippedProject component renders the embedded preview.
knownLimitations: Two fetches on mount; mitigated by parallelism. If capacity API is down, the badge shows a fallback state
Clock-aware greeting reads VISITOR's clock; an admin viewing from a different timezone might get a 'wrong-feeling' greeting. Cost is tiny
HeroShippedProject embeds the latest showcased project; if none exist, falls back to a static placeholder

statusHistory.reason: Component shipped with the offer + live capacity + greeting.
Code-first doc co-located. Live-capacity + clock-aware greeting stable.
